stories.headline: Takaichi's approval rating slips as glow from US-Japan summit fades
Bank of Japan’s ‘Hawkish Hold’ Strengthens Yen, Strategists Say
stories.summary: Bank of Japan’s decision to hold rates on Tuesday is seen as hawkish and contributed to the yen’s gains against the dollar, strategists say. The BOJ left its benchmark rate unchanged, with a split vote signaling increased odds of a hike in June, pushing the yen higher.

stories.headline: China’s State Refiners Seek to Resume Fuel Exports After Halt
stories.summary: China’s state-owned refiners have begun applying for government permits that would allow them to resume fuel exports in May, citing plentiful domestic stockpiles.

stories.headline: US-Sanctioned Chinese Refiner Hengli Restructures Singapore Unit
stories.summary: China’s Hengli Group Co. has changed the ownership structure of its Singapore-based oil trading arm after the US sanctioned its refining unit, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

stories.headline: Retail Investors Return as India’s Small-, Mid-Cap Stock Rally Builds
stories.summary: They have bought more than $1 billion worth of shares so far this month.

stories.headline: Why Is China Blocking Meta’s Acquisition of Manus?
stories.summary: China has decided to block Meta Platforms Inc.’s $2 billion acquisition of agentic AI startup Manus, a surprise move to unwind a controversial deal that’s drawn fire for the leakage of technology to the US. Bloomberg's Nectar Gan has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Nissan Shares Climb After Forecasting Profit Instead of Loss
stories.summary: Nissan Motor Co. shares climbed the most in more than two months after the Japanese carmaker raised its earnings outlook, avoiding what could have been its first annual operating loss in five years.

stories.headline: Korea Passes UK to Become World’s Eighth-Largest Stock Market
stories.summary: South Korea has leapfrogged the UK to become the world’s eighth-biggest stock market, fueled by a high-octane rally in its artificial intelligence-linked technology champions.

stories.headline: Advantest Shares Decline After Outlook Misses on Tight Capacity
stories.summary: Advantest Corp.’s stock dropped as much as 6.9% after the maker of AI chip testers gave a lackluster outlook and pointed to continued capacity constraints.

stories.headline: Israeli settlers attack Palestinians and set house on fire
stories.summary: Israeli settlers set fire to a Palestinian home in the occupied West Bank village of Jalud.

stories.headline: Merz suggests Ukraine’s EU membership may depend on territorial concessions
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said on Monday that Ukraine’s proposed timeline for EU membership is unrealistic, as debate over enlargement continues to intensify. “Zelenskyy had the idea of joining the EU on January 1, 2027. That will not work. Even January 1, 2028, is not realistic."

stories.headline: Iran FM in Russia says US has offered new round of talks
stories.summary: Russia is hosting Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in St. Petersburg for talks on a possible new round of talks.

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz standoff leaves 20,000 seafarers stranded on cargo ships
stories.summary: Thousands of seafarers are stranded as the Iran war shuts down much of the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting global oil and gas trade and raising safety fears.

stories.headline: Archbishop praises Pope's anti-war comments during Vatican visit

stories.headline: China’s Crowded Battery Market Weighs on Energy Storage Profits

stories.headline: Investigators in Trump Assassination Attempt Look Through Bluesky for Suspect’s Motive

stories.headline: China Outlook Raised to Stable by Moody’s Even as Debt Piles Up
stories.summary: Moody’s Ratings revised China’s credit outlook to stable from negative, an expression of confidence in the economy as the government copes with risks stemming from its swelling debt load.

stories.summary: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd., the world’s biggest battery maker, has raised $5 billion after pricing a Hong Kong share placement at the bottom of a marketed range.

stories.headline: Hongqiao Prices $1.5 Billion Convertible Sale Near Low End

stories.headline: Daiwa Buys Orix Bank for $2.3 Billion in Loans Push; Shares Fall

stories.headline: EU faces ‘China shock’ as EV imports drive Beijing’s record surplus with bloc
stories.summary: China sold goods worth about $148bn to EU in first quarter of year, but imported just $65bnThe EU is experiencing a prolonged “China shock” as a flood of Chinese EVs into Europe helped push Beijing to a record surplus with the bloc.New data showed China’s trade surplus – where its exports to the EU exceeded imports from the bloc – was $83bn (£61bn) in the first three months of 2026. stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
stories.summary: Two-up is a coin toss betting game that was played by Australian soldiers during World War One - it’s now only legal across Australia on 25 April.
